metadata.note: Output used as-is without manual revision or selection.

causal_texts: The glass slipped from Alice's hand. The glass fell to the floor. The glass broke into many pieces. Alice swept them up carefully.
The chef chopped the onions into small pieces. The chef heated olive oil in a large pan. The chef added the onions to the pan. The onions turned golden brown and soft.
The boy kicked the soccer ball very hard. The soccer ball sailed over the tall fence. The soccer ball landed in the neighbor's yard. The boy climbed the fence to get the soccer ball.
Dark clouds gathered over the small town. Heavy rain fell from the dark clouds. The streets of the town flooded with water. Workers pumped the water out of the streets.

The student opened the textbook to chapter five. The student read the chapter for two full hours. The student underlined the important passages in the chapter. The student wrote a summary of the chapter.
The robin collected twigs from the ground. The robin built a small nest in the oak tree. The robin laid three eggs in the nest. The three eggs hatched after two weeks.
The painter set up an easel in the park. The painter mixed bright colors on a palette. The painter applied the colors to a blank canvas. The finished canvas dried in the afternoon sun.
The farmer planted tomato seeds in the soil. The tomato seeds sprouted after one week. The tomato plants grew tall during the summer. The farmer picked ripe tomatoes from the tomato plants.
The children rolled a large ball of snow. The children stacked a smaller ball on top. The children added a carrot nose and button eyes. The snowman stood proudly in the front yard.
The dog spotted a stick near the pond. The dog ran toward the stick at full speed. The dog grabbed the stick with strong jaws. The dog carried the stick back to the owner.
The woman measured coffee grounds into the filter. The woman poured hot water over the coffee grounds. The coffee dripped slowly into the glass pot. The woman poured the coffee into a ceramic mug.
The car made a strange rattling noise. The driver pulled the car to the side of the road. The driver opened the hood of the car. The driver discovered a loose belt inside the engine.
Lightning struck the old barn during the storm. The old barn caught fire from the lightning strike. Flames spread across the roof of the old barn. The firefighters arrived and put out the fire.
The girl dropped the phone on the concrete sidewalk. The screen of the phone cracked from the impact. The girl picked the phone up from the sidewalk. The girl took the phone to a repair shop.
The cat crouched low in the tall grass. The cat spotted a small mouse near the fence. The cat leaped toward the small mouse quickly. The small mouse escaped into a hole in the fence.
The man placed two slices of bread on the counter. The man spread mustard on the two slices of bread. The man added lettuce and turkey to the bread. The man cut the sandwich in half with a knife.
The sailor untied the boat from the wooden dock. The sailor raised the white sail on the boat. The wind filled the white sail with a strong gust. The boat glided smoothly across the calm lake.
The child held the kite up in the air. A gust of wind caught the kite from behind. The kite rose high above the open field. The child held the string of the kite tightly.

The grandmother sat down at the writing desk. The grandmother wrote a long letter to a friend. The grandmother folded the letter and placed the letter in an envelope. The grandmother mailed the envelope at the post office.
The camper gathered dry sticks from the forest floor. The camper arranged the dry sticks in a small pile. The camper lit the pile of sticks with a match. A warm fire crackled in the stone fire pit.

The mother filled the kitchen sink with soapy water. The mother placed the dirty plates in the soapy water. The mother scrubbed each plate with a sponge. The mother placed the clean plates on the drying rack.
The ice cream cone sat in the hot sun. The ice cream began to melt and drip. The melted ice cream ran down the cone. A sticky puddle of ice cream formed on the table.

The train approached the crowded station at noon. The train slowed down and stopped at the platform. The passengers stepped off the train onto the platform. The conductor closed the doors of the train.

The woman walked through the aisles of the grocery store. The woman selected fresh vegetables and placed the vegetables in a cart. The woman paid for the vegetables at the checkout counter. The woman loaded the grocery bags into the car.
The fisherman cast the fishing line into the river. A large trout bit the hook on the fishing line. The fisherman reeled the large trout in slowly. The fisherman placed the large trout in a cooler.

The photographer set up a camera on a tripod. The photographer adjusted the lens of the camera carefully. The photographer pressed the shutter button on the camera. The camera captured a beautiful image of the sunset.

The tailor measured the fabric with a tape measure. The tailor cut the fabric along the marked lines. The tailor sewed the pieces of fabric together. The tailor pressed the finished garment with a hot iron.
The potter placed a lump of clay on the wheel. The potter shaped the clay into a tall vase. The potter placed the tall vase in the kiln. The tall vase hardened in the kiln overnight.
The pianist sat down on the bench at the piano. The pianist placed both hands on the piano keys. The pianist played a slow and beautiful melody. The audience in the hall applauded the pianist warmly.
The hikers started at the base of the steep hill. The hikers climbed the narrow trail for two hours. The hikers reached the top of the steep hill. The hikers enjoyed a wide view of the valley.
The swimmer stood at the edge of the pool. The swimmer dove into the deep end of the pool. The swimmer swam ten laps across the pool. The swimmer climbed out of the pool and dried off.
The baker measured flour and sugar into a bowl. The baker stirred eggs and butter into the bowl. The baker poured the batter into a round pan. The baker placed the round pan in the hot oven.
The father drove the car to the car wash. The father sprayed water on the car with a hose. The father scrubbed the car with a large sponge. The car looked shiny and clean after the wash.

The painter taped off the edges of the bedroom wall. The painter poured blue paint into a wide tray. The painter rolled the blue paint onto the bedroom wall. The bedroom wall dried to a smooth blue finish.

The mechanic examined the flat tire on the bicycle. The mechanic removed the flat tire from the bicycle wheel. The mechanic patched the hole in the flat tire. The mechanic reattached the repaired tire to the bicycle wheel.

The gardener pushed the lawn mower out of the shed. The gardener started the lawn mower with a pull cord. The gardener mowed the entire front lawn in rows. The gardener put the lawn mower back in the shed.

The carpenter measured the wooden boards with a ruler. The carpenter cut the wooden boards to the right length. The carpenter nailed the wooden boards together with a hammer. The carpenter sanded the finished bookshelf until the surface was smooth.

The child picked out shiny wrapping paper from the closet. The child wrapped the birthday gift in the wrapping paper. The child tied a red ribbon around the wrapped gift. The child placed the wrapped gift on the party table.

The girl squeezed six lemons into a large glass pitcher. The girl added cold water and sugar to the pitcher. The girl stirred the lemonade in the pitcher with a spoon. The girl poured the lemonade into cups with ice.

The commuter waited at the bus stop in the rain. The bus arrived at the bus stop five minutes late. The commuter stepped onto the bus and paid the fare. The bus carried the commuter downtown to the office.

The man searched the entire house for the car keys. The man found the car keys under a couch cushion. The man grabbed the car keys and walked to the door. The man drove to work ten minutes behind schedule.

The office worker carried a full cup of coffee carefully. The office worker tripped on a loose cable on the floor. The cup of coffee spilled all over the worker's desk. The office worker wiped the desk clean with paper towels.

The programmer typed new code into the text editor. The programmer ran the new code on the computer. The computer displayed an error message on the screen. The programmer found and fixed the mistake in the new code.

The secretary clicked the print button on the computer screen. The printer received the document from the computer. The printer produced ten pages of the document. The secretary collected the ten pages from the printer tray.

The traveler folded clothes and placed the clothes in a suitcase. The traveler zipped the suitcase shut on the bed. The traveler carried the suitcase down the stairs. The traveler loaded the suitcase into the trunk of the taxi.

The baker spread white frosting on top of the cake. The baker placed strawberries on the white frosting. The baker added a ring of chocolate sprinkles around the edge. The decorated cake looked beautiful on the glass stand.

The father cut the top off a large orange pumpkin. The father scooped the seeds out of the large orange pumpkin. The father carved a face into the side of the pumpkin. The father placed a candle inside the carved pumpkin.

The children dug a big hole in the wet sand. The children built tall walls around the hole in the sand. The children added a moat around the sand castle walls. A large wave washed the sand castle away.

The janitor filled a bucket with warm soapy water. The janitor dipped a squeegee into the bucket of water. The janitor wiped every window on the first floor. The windows on the first floor sparkled in the sunlight.

The mother heated the iron on the ironing board. The mother spread a wrinkled shirt flat on the board. The mother pressed the hot iron across the wrinkled shirt. The wrinkled shirt became smooth and crisp.

The farmer opened the gate of the chicken coop. The farmer scattered grain on the ground near the coop. The chickens rushed out and pecked at the grain. The farmer refilled the water trough inside the chicken coop.
The farmer led the cow into the milking barn. The farmer placed a clean pail under the cow. The farmer milked the cow by hand into the pail. The farmer carried the full pail of milk to the kitchen.

The boy climbed the ladder to reach the apple tree branches. The boy picked red apples from the apple tree branches. The boy placed the red apples in a woven basket. The boy climbed down the ladder with the full basket.

The grandmother chose soft blue yarn from the basket. The grandmother cast stitches onto the knitting needles. The grandmother knitted rows of blue yarn every evening. The grandmother finished a warm scarf after three weeks.

The boy folded a sheet of paper into a pointed shape. The boy held the paper airplane above the boy's head. The boy threw the paper airplane across the school yard. The paper airplane glided and landed near the swings.

The clown inflated a long red balloon with a pump. The clown twisted the red balloon into loops and bends. The clown shaped the red balloon into a poodle figure. The clown handed the balloon poodle to the small child.

The scout found a flat clearing in the forest. The scout unpacked the tent from a large backpack. The scout pushed the tent poles into the soft ground. The scout stretched the tent fabric over the poles.

The camper cleared a circle of dirt near the campsite. The camper stacked logs and kindling in the dirt circle. The camper struck a match and lit the kindling. The campfire grew and warmed the area around the campsite.

The host boiled water in a kettle on the stove. The host placed loose tea leaves in a ceramic teapot. The host poured the boiling water into the ceramic teapot. The host served the tea in small porcelain cups.

The customer looked at the menu on the restaurant wall. The customer ordered a bowl of soup at the counter. The cook prepared the bowl of soup in the kitchen. The customer carried the bowl of soup to a table.

The tourist aimed the camera at the old stone bridge. The tourist adjusted the focus of the camera lens. The tourist pressed the shutter button and captured the image. The tourist reviewed the image on the camera's small screen.

The art student sharpened a pencil to a fine point. The art student sketched the outline of a horse on paper. The art student added shading and detail to the horse drawing. The art student signed the finished horse drawing in the corner.

The mother pulled the warm laundry from the dryer. The mother sorted the warm laundry into separate piles. The mother folded each piece of laundry neatly. The mother placed the folded laundry in the dresser drawers.

The soldier wiped dirt from the surface of the leather boots. The soldier applied black polish to the leather boots. The soldier buffed the leather boots with a soft cloth. The leather boots gleamed under the bright light.

The cobbler examined the worn sole of the old shoe. The cobbler removed the worn sole with a sharp blade. The cobbler glued a new sole onto the old shoe. The cobbler stitched the new sole firmly into place.

The bartender placed a tall glass on the counter. The bartender poured orange juice into the tall glass. The bartender added ice cubes to the tall glass. The bartender slid the tall glass across the counter to the customer.

The boy tied a strong knot at the end of the rope. The boy looped the rope over a high tree branch. The boy pulled the rope tight against the tree branch. The boy attached a wooden plank to make a rope swing.
The worker drilled a hole in the center of the wall. The worker pushed a plastic anchor into the hole. The worker drove a screw into the plastic anchor. The worker hung a framed picture on the screw.

The plumber turned off the water supply to the bathtub. The plumber removed the old faucet from the bathtub. The plumber installed a new faucet on the bathtub. The plumber turned the water supply back on.
The mother swept the kitchen floor with a straw broom. The mother collected a pile of dust and crumbs. The mother scooped the pile into a dustpan. The mother emptied the dustpan into the trash can.

Autumn leaves fell from the trees onto the front lawn. The homeowner raked the autumn leaves into large piles. The homeowner stuffed the autumn leaves into paper bags. The homeowner placed the paper bags at the curb for pickup.

A heavy snowstorm covered the driveway in deep snow. The neighbor grabbed a shovel from the garage. The neighbor shoveled the deep snow off the driveway. The neighbor spread salt on the cleared driveway.
The dog dug a deep hole in the backyard garden. The dog buried a large bone in the deep hole. The dog pushed the dirt back over the large bone. The dog walked away from the spot in the garden.

The lumberjack chose a tall pine tree in the forest. The lumberjack swung an axe at the base of the pine tree. The pine tree cracked and fell to the ground. The lumberjack cut the pine tree into shorter logs.

The angler waded into the shallow part of the stream. The angler cast a fly into the deeper water downstream. A brown trout struck the fly with a quick bite. The angler pulled the brown trout out of the stream.

The captain steered the sailboat away from the harbor. The wind picked up and filled the sails of the sailboat. The sailboat raced across the open water at high speed. The captain anchored the sailboat near a sandy island.

The rider placed a saddle on the back of the horse. The rider mounted the horse from the left side. The rider guided the horse along a dirt trail. The horse and the rider returned to the stable at sunset.

The boy found a sturdy branch on the ground near the creek. The boy used the sturdy branch to cross the wide creek. The boy stepped carefully on the rocks in the wide creek. The boy reached the other side of the wide creek safely.

The engineer drew the plans for a small wooden bridge. The construction crew built the frame of the wooden bridge. The construction crew laid thick planks across the frame. The small wooden bridge opened to foot traffic the same week.

The scientist placed a model rocket on the launch pad. The scientist connected the ignition wires to the model rocket. The scientist pressed the launch button on the controller. The model rocket shot high into the clear blue sky.

The teacher wrote a math problem on the chalkboard. The teacher asked the students to solve the math problem. The students worked on the math problem at the desks. One student walked to the chalkboard and wrote the answer.

The actors rehearsed the scene on the empty stage. The stage crew adjusted the lights above the stage. The curtain rose in front of a full audience. The actors performed the scene without a single mistake.

The conductor raised the baton above the orchestra. The orchestra began to play the opening notes of the symphony. The music filled the large concert hall with sound. The audience remained silent until the final note ended.

The sprinter crouched at the starting line on the track. The starting pistol fired with a loud bang. The sprinter burst forward off the starting blocks. The sprinter crossed the finish line in first place.

The striker received a pass near the goal on the field. The striker dribbled past the last defender quickly. The striker kicked the ball toward the bottom left corner. The ball flew past the goalkeeper and into the net.

The athlete trained for the marathon for six long months. The athlete ran in the marathon on a cool morning. The athlete passed the twenty-mile mark feeling strong. The athlete crossed the finish line in under four hours.

The detective found a clue at the scene of the crime. The detective followed the clue to a nearby warehouse. The detective discovered more evidence inside the warehouse. The detective solved the case based on the collected evidence.

The old man spread a map on the kitchen table. The old man marked a spot on the map with a red pen. The old man walked to the marked spot in the field. The old man dug into the ground and found a metal box.

The girl heard a kitten crying under the porch. The girl crawled under the porch on hands and knees. The girl found a tiny kitten trapped behind a board. The girl gently pulled the tiny kitten free from the board.

The runner jogged slowly during the first mile of the race. The runner picked up speed during the second mile. The runner sprinted hard during the final stretch of the race. The runner collapsed on the grass after crossing the finish line.

The beekeeper put on a protective suit and gloves. The beekeeper opened the lid of the wooden beehive. The beekeeper removed a frame covered with honeycomb. The beekeeper scraped the honey from the honeycomb into a jar.

The librarian noticed a torn page in the old book. The librarian applied thin adhesive tape to the torn page. The librarian pressed the torn edges of the page together. The librarian returned the repaired book to the library shelf.

The plumber noticed water dripping from the kitchen pipe. The plumber turned off the water valve under the kitchen sink. The plumber replaced the cracked section of the kitchen pipe. The plumber turned the water valve back on to test the repair.

The electrician turned off the power at the circuit breaker. The electrician removed the old light switch from the wall. The electrician connected the wires to a new light switch. The electrician turned the power back on at the circuit breaker.

The blacksmith heated a bar of iron in the hot forge. The bar of iron glowed bright orange in the forge. The blacksmith hammered the bar of iron on the anvil. The blacksmith shaped the bar of iron into a horseshoe.

The mailman sorted the letters at the post office early. The mailman loaded the letters into a delivery truck. The mailman drove the delivery truck through every neighborhood. The mailman placed the letters into the mailboxes along the route.

The archaeologist brushed sand away from a buried object. The archaeologist uncovered the edge of an ancient clay pot. The archaeologist carefully lifted the ancient clay pot from the ground. The archaeologist placed the ancient clay pot in a padded crate.

The waiter carried a tray of glasses to the dining table. The waiter tripped on the edge of a rug near the table. The tray of glasses fell and crashed on the hard floor. The waiter cleaned up the broken glass with a dustpan.

The pilot checked the instruments in the cockpit before takeoff. The pilot taxied the airplane down the long runway. The pilot pulled back on the controls at the end of the runway. The airplane lifted off the ground and climbed into the sky.

The child placed a coin into the slot of the vending machine. The child pressed the button for a chocolate bar. The vending machine dropped the chocolate bar to the bottom tray. The child reached into the bottom tray and grabbed the chocolate bar.
noncausal_texts: A violin is played with a bow. A flute is played by blowing air. A drum is played by striking it. A harp is played by plucking strings.
Jupiter had a massive swirling red storm. Neptune experienced the strongest winds in the solar system. Mars had two small rocky moons. Saturn was surrounded by thousands of visible rings.
The Arctic fox had thick white fur in winter. The fennec fox had unusually large ears. The red fox lived in forests across Europe and Asia. The gray fox climbed trees to escape predators.

Cairo served as the capital of Egypt for centuries. Canberra was chosen as the capital of Australia in 1908. Brasilia was built as the capital of Brazil in 1960. Ottawa was selected as the capital of Canada in 1857.

Flamingos got the pink color from the food flamingos ate. Peacocks displayed large colorful tail feathers during mating season. Cardinals kept the same bright red plumage all year. Toucans had bills colored with orange, green, and red.

The Caspian Sea was the largest enclosed body of water. Lake Titicaca sat high in the Andes Mountains. The Dead Sea had the saltiest water of any lake. Lake Baikal held more fresh water than any other lake.

Mount Kilimanjaro rose from flat plains in Tanzania. Mount Denali was the tallest peak in North America. The Matterhorn had a distinctive pyramid-shaped summit. Mount Fuji was a symmetrical volcanic cone in Japan.

Baklava was a pastry made with layers of filo dough. Mochi was a Japanese dessert made from pounded rice. Churros were fried dough sticks coated in cinnamon sugar. Tiramisu was an Italian dessert made with coffee and mascarpone.

The baobab tree stored water inside a massive trunk. The bristlecone pine lived for thousands of years. The banyan tree spread wide by dropping roots from branches. The Joshua tree grew only in the Mojave Desert.

Copper conducted electricity and resisted corrosion well. Titanium was extremely strong but very lightweight. Zinc protected other metals from rusting when applied as coating. Tin was soft and melted at a low temperature.

Sapphires came in blue, pink, and yellow varieties. Opals displayed shifting colors depending on the viewing angle. Emeralds got the green color from trace amounts of chromium. Rubies and sapphires were both forms of the mineral corundum.

Table tennis originated in England during the Victorian era. Lacrosse developed from a game played by Native American peoples. Badminton evolved from a game played in British India. Judo was created in Japan in the late nineteenth century.

The waltz originated in Austria during the eighteenth century. The samba developed in Brazil from African dance traditions. The flamenco had roots in the Andalusia region of Spain. The tango emerged in the working-class neighborhoods of Buenos Aires.

Mandarin Chinese had the most native speakers of any language. Icelandic changed very little over the past thousand years. Basque was unrelated to any other known living language. Tamil was one of the oldest continuously used written languages.

The Swiss franc served as the currency of Switzerland. The Kenyan shilling was the official currency of Kenya. The Polish zloty was the currency used in Poland. The Thai baht served as the official currency of Thailand.

Silk was produced from the cocoons of silkworms. Linen was woven from the fibers of the flax plant. Cashmere came from the soft undercoat of cashmere goats. Angora was made from the fur of angora rabbits.

Saffron came from the dried stigmas of crocus flowers. Cardamom grew inside small green pods on tropical plants. Vanilla was extracted from the seed pods of orchid vines. Cinnamon was harvested from the inner bark of certain trees.

Pumpernickel bread was a dense dark bread from Westphalia. Naan was a flatbread baked in a tandoor clay oven. Injera was a spongy flatbread made from teff flour. Focaccia was an Italian bread seasoned with olive oil and salt.

Gruyere was a hard Swiss cheese with a nutty flavor. Feta was a brined Greek cheese made from sheep milk. Manchego was a Spanish cheese aged for several months. Brie was a soft French cheese with a white rind.

Lavender produced fragrant purple flowers on tall stems. The lotus flower grew in shallow muddy ponds and lakes. Sunflowers turned the flower heads to follow the path of sunlight. Jasmine released a strong sweet scent during the night.

The emperor penguin lived in colonies on Antarctic ice. The kiwi bird was a flightless bird native to New Zealand. The pelican had a large pouch beneath the pelican's long bill. The hummingbird flapped the hummingbird's wings up to eighty times per second.

The anglerfish used a glowing lure to attract prey. The flying fish leaped out of the water and glided through air. The seahorse was the only fish where the male carried the eggs. The pufferfish inflated into a round ball when threatened.

The rhinoceros beetle carried objects many times the beetle's own weight. The monarch butterfly migrated thousands of miles each autumn. The praying mantis turned the mantis's head a full 180 degrees. The firefly produced cold light through a chemical reaction in the abdomen.

The chameleon changed skin color to match the surroundings. The Komodo dragon was the largest living species of lizard. The gecko walked on smooth vertical surfaces using tiny toe pads. The green sea turtle migrated long distances between feeding grounds and nesting beaches.

The blue whale was the largest animal that ever lived. The platypus laid eggs despite being classified as a mammal. The sloth moved very slowly and slept up to twenty hours daily. The bat was the only mammal capable of sustained flight.

Artichokes were the edible flower buds of a thistle plant. Kohlrabi was a bulbous vegetable in the cabbage family. Daikon was a long white radish popular in East Asian cooking. Okra produced seed pods used in stews and soups.

Pomegranates contained hundreds of small red seeds inside. Persimmons turned sweet only after the fruit ripened fully. Lychees had a rough pink shell covering translucent white flesh. Guavas had green skin and soft pink flesh with many seeds.

Amaranth was an ancient grain grown by the Aztec people. Teff was a tiny grain native to the highlands of Ethiopia. Millet thrived in dry and arid growing conditions. Farro was an ancient wheat grain used in Italian cuisine.

Macadamia nuts had a hard shell that required a special cracker. Pine nuts came from the cones of certain pine tree species. Pistachios grew in clusters on small desert trees. Brazil nuts grew inside large round pods in the Amazon rainforest.

Yerba mate was a caffeinated drink brewed from dried holly leaves. Horchata was a sweet rice-based drink popular in Mexico and Spain. Lassi was a yogurt-based drink from the Indian subcontinent. Kvass was a fermented beverage made from rye bread in Russia.

Miso soup was a Japanese soup made from fermented soybean paste. Borscht was a beet-based soup popular in Eastern European countries. Tom yum was a hot and sour soup from Thailand. Gazpacho was a cold tomato soup served in Spanish cuisine.

A wrench was a tool used to grip and turn bolts. A chisel was a tool used to carve wood or stone. An awl was a pointed tool used to punch holes in leather. A plane was a tool used to shave thin layers from wood.

Blues music developed in the southern United States from African traditions. Reggae music originated on the island of Jamaica in the 1960s. Bossa nova blended samba rhythms with jazz harmonies in Brazil. Fado was a melancholic music genre from Lisbon and Coimbra in Portugal.

Impressionist painters used visible brushstrokes and bright pure colors. Cubist artists depicted subjects from multiple viewpoints at once. Surrealist painters combined dreamlike images with realistic detail. Art Nouveau designers used flowing organic lines inspired by natural forms.

The Parthenon in Athens used the Doric column style. The Taj Mahal combined elements of Persian and Mughal architecture. The Sydney Opera House featured a series of sail-shaped roof shells. Fallingwater was a house built over a waterfall in Pennsylvania.

The Golden Gate Bridge was a suspension bridge in San Francisco. The Rialto Bridge was a stone arch bridge in Venice. The Tower Bridge in London had two tall Gothic-style towers. The Charles Bridge in Prague was decorated with thirty baroque statues.

The Leaning Tower of Pisa tilted because of soft ground underneath. The CN Tower in Toronto was one of the tallest freestanding structures. The Eiffel Tower was built as a temporary exhibit for a world fair. Big Ben was the nickname for the large bell inside the Elizabeth Tower.

Neuschwanstein Castle stood on a rugged hill in Bavaria. The Alhambra was a Moorish palace complex in Granada, Spain. Edinburgh Castle sat on top of an ancient volcanic rock. Himeji Castle in Japan was known for the brilliant white exterior walls.

The Library of Congress held the largest collection of books in the world. The Bodleian Library at Oxford opened in the year 1602. The Royal Library of Alexandria was one of the most famous ancient libraries. The British Library stored over 170 million items in the collection.

The Louvre Museum in Paris was once a royal palace. The Hermitage Museum in Saint Petersburg occupied six historic buildings. The Smithsonian Institution in Washington operated nineteen separate museums. The Prado Museum in Madrid held a vast collection of European paintings.

Yellowstone National Park sat on top of a large volcanic caldera. The Serengeti National Park was famous for the annual wildebeest migration. Banff National Park was the oldest national park in Canada. Torres del Paine National Park had dramatic granite peaks in Patagonia.

The Sahara Desert covered most of northern Africa. The Atacama Desert in Chile received almost no rainfall. The Gobi Desert stretched across southern Mongolia and northern China. The Kalahari Desert covered parts of Botswana, Namibia, and South Africa.

The Black Forest was a dense wooded region in southwestern Germany. The Amazon Rainforest produced a large share of the world's oxygen. The boreal forest stretched across northern Canada and Scandinavia. Sherwood Forest in England was associated with the legend of Robin Hood.

The Nile River flowed northward through northeastern Africa. The Danube River passed through ten different European countries. The Mekong River supported millions of people in Southeast Asia. The Yangtze River was the longest river on the Asian continent.

Lake Victoria was the largest lake in Africa by surface area. Lake Geneva sat on the border between Switzerland and France. The Great Salt Lake in Utah had no outlet to the ocean. Lake Malawi contained more species of fish than any other lake.

Madagascar was the fourth largest island in the world. Greenland was covered by a massive ice sheet. Borneo was divided among three different countries. Iceland had active volcanoes and large glaciers on the same island.

The Yucatan Peninsula extended into the Gulf of Mexico. The Iberian Peninsula contained both Spain and Portugal. The Korean Peninsula extended southward from the Asian mainland. The Scandinavian Peninsula was shared by Norway and Sweden.

Mount Vesuvius erupted and buried the city of Pompeii. Mount Saint Helens erupted in Washington State in 1980. Mount Krakatoa produced one of the loudest explosions in recorded history. Mount Pinatubo's eruption cooled global temperatures for several years.

The printing press was invented in Germany around the year 1440. The telephone was patented in the United States in 1876. The first practical automobile was built in Germany in 1885. The World Wide Web was invented at CERN in 1989.

Marie Curie discovered the elements radium and polonium. Nikola Tesla developed the alternating current electrical system. Rosalind Franklin produced crucial images of the DNA structure. Dmitri Mendeleev organized the chemical elements into the periodic table.

Roald Amundsen was the first person to reach the South Pole. Ibn Battuta traveled across Africa, Asia, and Europe in the 1300s. Sacagawea guided the Lewis and Clark expedition across western lands. Vasco da Gama sailed from Portugal to India around the African coast.

Leo Tolstoy wrote lengthy novels about Russian society and war. Murasaki Shikibu wrote one of the world's first novels in Japan. Jorge Luis Borges wrote short stories that explored labyrinths and infinity. Chinua Achebe wrote novels about life in Nigeria during colonial rule.

Frida Kahlo painted vivid self-portraits influenced by Mexican folk art. Katsushika Hokusai created famous woodblock prints of landscapes and waves. Georgia O'Keeffe painted large-scale close-up images of flowers and bones. Diego Rivera painted enormous murals depicting Mexican history and culture.

Beethoven composed the Ninth Symphony while almost completely deaf. Hildegard von Bingen composed liturgical music in the twelfth century. Fela Kuti created the Afrobeat genre in Nigeria during the 1970s. Ravi Shankar brought Indian classical sitar music to Western audiences.

Confucius taught ideas about ethics and proper social conduct in China. Simone de Beauvoir wrote influential works on feminism and existentialism. Al-Farabi wrote commentaries on the works of Aristotle in Baghdad. John Locke argued that government required the consent of the governed.

Hatshepsut ruled Egypt as pharaoh during the fifteenth century BCE. Mansa Musa was one of the wealthiest rulers in recorded history. Catherine the Great expanded the borders of the Russian Empire greatly. Emperor Meiji oversaw the rapid modernization of Japan.

The Battle of Thermopylae was fought at a narrow mountain pass. The Battle of Hastings took place on the coast of southern England. The Battle of Gettysburg lasted for three days in Pennsylvania. The Battle of Trafalgar was a naval engagement fought off the coast of Spain.

The Treaty of Westphalia ended the Thirty Years War in 1648. The Treaty of Tordesillas divided new lands between Spain and Portugal. The Treaty of Versailles imposed harsh terms on Germany after the war. The Treaty of Nanking opened Chinese ports to British trade.

Diwali was a festival of lights celebrated across South Asia. Hanami was the Japanese tradition of viewing cherry blossoms in spring. Day of the Dead honored deceased family members in Mexican culture. Songkran marked the traditional new year celebration in Thailand.

Chess originated in India and spread along ancient trade routes. Mancala was one of the oldest known board games in Africa. Go was a strategy game played in China for over 2500 years. Backgammon was a board game that combined strategy and dice rolls.

Krav Maga was a self-defense system developed in Israel. Capoeira combined martial arts and dance elements from Brazil. Taekwondo emphasized high kicks and fast spinning techniques. Aikido used the attacker's own force to redirect and control movement.

The decathlon consisted of ten different track and field events. Synchronized swimming combined athletic skill with artistic choreography. The biathlon combined cross-country skiing with rifle marksmanship. Water polo was a team sport played in a swimming pool.

The Labrador retriever was originally bred to help fishermen. The Shiba Inu was an ancient hunting breed from mountainous Japan. The Rhodesian ridgeback was bred to track lions in southern Africa. The Dalmatian was historically used to guard horse-drawn carriages.

The Maine Coon was one of the largest domestic cat breeds. The Siamese cat had a distinctive loud and vocal personality. The Scottish Fold had ears that bent forward and downward. The Sphynx cat lacked a fur coat and felt warm to the touch.

The Arabian horse was prized for speed and endurance in desert conditions. The Clydesdale was a tall draft horse bred for heavy farm work. The Fjord horse was a small sturdy breed from western Norway. The Appaloosa was known for a distinctive spotted coat pattern.

Highland cattle had long shaggy coats and curved wide horns. Brahman cattle thrived in hot and humid tropical climates. Hereford cattle had distinctive red bodies with white faces. Angus cattle were a popular breed raised for high-quality beef.

Braising involved cooking food slowly in a small amount of liquid. Poaching involved cooking food gently in water just below the boiling point. Searing involved cooking food quickly at very high temperatures. Blanching involved boiling food briefly and plunging the food in ice water.

Penne was a short tube-shaped pasta cut at an angle. Orecchiette was a small ear-shaped pasta from southern Italy. Farfalle was a bow-tie-shaped pasta with pinched edges. Rigatoni was a large ridged tube-shaped pasta from Rome.

Pu-erh tea was aged and fermented in the Yunnan province of China. Matcha tea was ground into a fine green powder in Japan. Rooibos tea came from a plant native to South Africa. Darjeeling tea was grown in the foothills of the Himalayan mountains.

Turkish coffee was brewed in a small pot called a cezve. Ethiopian coffee was roasted in a pan during a traditional ceremony. Vietnamese coffee was brewed slowly through a small metal filter. Cuban coffee was sweetened with sugar during the brewing process.

Prosecco was a sparkling wine produced in the Veneto region of Italy. Riesling was a white wine grape grown widely in the Rhine region. Malbec was a red wine grape that thrived in Argentine vineyards. Sauvignon Blanc was a crisp white wine from the Loire Valley.

A stout was a dark beer brewed with roasted barley and malt. A pilsner was a pale golden lager that originated in Bohemia. A hefeweizen was a cloudy wheat beer from southern Germany. A lambic was a Belgian beer fermented with wild yeast.

The margarita was a cocktail made with tequila, lime, and orange liqueur. The caipirinha was a Brazilian cocktail made with cachaca and lime. The negroni was an Italian cocktail made with gin, vermouth, and bitters. The mojito was a Cuban cocktail made with rum, mint, and lime.

The banh mi was a Vietnamese sandwich served on a crusty baguette. The shawarma was a Middle Eastern sandwich filled with roasted meat. The katsu sando was a Japanese sandwich filled with breaded pork cutlet. The cubano was a pressed sandwich with roasted pork and Swiss cheese.

Fattoush was a Lebanese salad made with toasted pieces of pita bread. Som tam was a spicy green papaya salad from Thailand. Nicoise salad was a French salad with tuna, eggs, and olives. Tabbouleh was a Levantine salad made with parsley, bulgur, and tomato.

Goulash was a Hungarian stew seasoned with paprika and caraway seeds. Tagine was a North African stew cooked in a cone-shaped clay pot. Pozole was a Mexican stew made with hominy corn and meat. Gumbo was a thick stew from Louisiana made with okra and roux.

The golden ratio appeared in the proportions of many natural forms. The number zero was first used as a placeholder in Babylonian mathematics. Pi was the ratio of a circle's circumference to the diameter. Fibonacci numbers appeared in the spiral patterns of sunflower seeds and pinecones.

Helium was the second lightest element on the periodic table. Neon glowed with a bright red-orange color inside glass tubes. Mercury was the only metal that existed as a liquid at room temperature. Tungsten had the highest melting point of all metallic elements.

Obsidian was a glossy volcanic glass formed from rapidly cooled lava. Pumice was a volcanic rock light enough to float on water. Sandstone was a sedimentary rock formed from compressed grains of sand. Marble was a metamorphic rock formed from limestone under heat and pressure.

Cumulus clouds were fluffy white clouds with flat gray bases. Cirrus clouds were thin wispy clouds found at very high altitudes. Nimbostratus clouds were thick dark clouds that produced steady rain. Lenticular clouds formed smooth lens-shaped discs over mountain peaks.

The mistral was a strong cold wind that blew through southern France. The chinook was a warm dry wind that descended the Rocky Mountains. The sirocco was a hot wind that carried Saharan dust across the Mediterranean. The monsoon brought heavy seasonal rains to South and Southeast Asia.

The Gulf Stream carried warm water from the Gulf of Mexico northeastward. The Humboldt Current brought cold water along the western coast of South America. The Kuroshio Current carried warm water past the eastern coast of Japan. The Antarctic Circumpolar Current flowed around the entire continent of Antarctica.

Spring tides produced the highest and lowest tides of the month. Neap tides produced the smallest tidal range of the month. Tidal bores sent waves traveling upstream in certain funnel-shaped rivers. Tide pools formed in rocky depressions along the ocean shoreline.

Australia experienced summer during the months of December and January. India experienced a monsoon season from June through September. Alaska experienced very long days during the summer solstice period. Norway experienced months of continuous darkness during the polar winter.

The Hebrew calendar followed a lunisolar cycle of months and years. The Islamic calendar was based purely on the cycles of the moon. The Ethiopian calendar had thirteen months in each calendar year. The Mayan calendar used interlocking cycles of different lengths.

Chinese characters represented words or ideas as individual symbols. The Cyrillic alphabet was used for Russian, Bulgarian, and Serbian writing. The Arabic script was written from right to left across the page. The Devanagari script was used to write Hindi, Sanskrit, and Marathi.

Roman numerals used letters to represent numerical values. The binary system represented all numbers using only zeros and ones. The Mayan number system used a base-twenty counting method. The Babylonian number system used a base-sixty counting structure.

A furlong was a unit of distance equal to one eighth of a mile. A fathom was a unit of depth equal to six feet of water. A hectare was a metric unit of area equal to ten thousand square meters. A bushel was a unit of volume used to measure dry goods like grain.

Japan Standard Time was nine hours ahead of Coordinated Universal Time. India used a single time zone with a thirty-minute offset. China used one official time zone for the entire country. Nepal's time zone had an unusual fifteen-minute offset from neighboring zones.

The constellation Orion was visible from both hemispheres of the Earth. The Southern Cross was visible only from the Southern Hemisphere. Ursa Major contained the well-known pattern called the Big Dipper. Cassiopeia formed a distinctive W-shape in the northern night sky.

Halley's Comet appeared in the sky roughly every seventy-six years. The Hale-Bopp comet was visible to the naked eye for eighteen months. Comet Shoemaker-Levy 9 broke apart and struck the planet Jupiter. The Rosetta spacecraft orbited and studied a comet up close.

Europa was an icy moon of Jupiter with a subsurface ocean. Titan was the largest moon of Saturn and had a thick atmosphere. Ganymede was the largest moon in the entire solar system. Triton orbited Neptune in the opposite direction of Neptune's rotation.

Sirius was the brightest star visible in the Earth's night sky. Betelgeuse was a red giant star in the constellation Orion. Proxima Centauri was the nearest star to the solar system. Polaris served as the North Star for celestial navigation.

The Milky Way was a spiral galaxy containing the solar system. The Andromeda Galaxy was the nearest large galaxy to the Milky Way. The Sombrero Galaxy had a bright nucleus and a wide dust lane. The Whirlpool Galaxy had prominent and well-defined spiral arms.

Geothermal energy came from heat stored deep within the Earth. Tidal energy was generated from the rise and fall of ocean water. Biomass energy was produced by burning organic plant and animal material. Hydroelectric energy was generated by flowing water turning large turbines.

Gondolas were flat-bottomed boats used in the canals of Venice. Junks were wooden sailing ships used in the waters of East Asia. Kayaks were small narrow boats originally used by the Inuit people. Dhows were traditional sailing vessels used in the Indian Ocean.

The abacus was an ancient calculating device used across many cultures. The astrolabe was an instrument used to measure the position of stars. The sextant was a navigation tool used to determine latitude at sea. The sundial was a timekeeping device that used the shadow cast by sunlight.

Terracotta was a reddish-brown clay material used for pottery and sculpture. Porcelain was a fine white ceramic material first produced in China. Stoneware was a dense ceramic material fired at very high temperatures. Earthenware was a porous ceramic material fired at relatively low temperatures.

The accordion was a bellows-driven instrument popular in folk music. The sitar had a long neck and resonating gourd used in Indian music. The bagpipes used an air-filled bag to produce a continuous droning sound. The didgeridoo was a wooden wind instrument developed by Aboriginal Australians.

The Appalachian Trail stretched over two thousand miles across eastern mountains. The Camino de Santiago was a pilgrimage route ending in northwestern Spain. The Inca Trail led through mountain passes to the ruins of Machu Picchu. The Silk Road connected trading cities across Asia and the Middle East.
